Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex The attackers utilize a multi-stage approach to disrupt transportation systems, starting with reconnaissance, resource development, and initial access, followed by execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and finally impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Process Scheduled Task OS Process Network Traffic User Account Target System Artifact File DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility Process Creation (Mechanism) PowerShell Script Application Log Content (Mechanism) File Metadata (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) System Information Discovery Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about transportation systems using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers monitor network traffic between nodes of a public or multicast network to capture sensitive information.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attackers create custom malware to exploit identified vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers use drive-by downloads to infect transportation system employees' devices with malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to run at specific times to execute malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers inject malware into running processes to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers utilize WMI to create persistent backdoors.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attackers obfuscate malware code to evade detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to brute force credentials using stolen login attempts.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers gather system information to identify key targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attackers use valid accounts to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect data from local systems to exfiltrate.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate data over alternative protocols to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt transportation systems by causing denial-of-service attacks on critical infrastructure.

Scenario.summary: The attackers employ a complex attack strategy involving multiple stages to disrupt transportation systems, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
